description: `ggen receipt verify` against the real, live sync receipt at .ggen-v2/receipt.json reports a valid, cryptographically signed BLAKE3 chain hash. Proven both pre- and post-frontmatter-fix, plus the full multi-record chain via `ggen receipt history`.
note: Receipt cryptographic validity is independent of generated-code correctness -- the receipt records the sync pipeline's own I/O (files written + their hashes), not whether the written Rust compiles. Related, out-of-scope observation: the receipt's per-file admission decisions ('reason': 'written', 'observed_outcome': 'Pass') were emitted for the pristine pack's broken (frontmatter-leaked, non-compiling) output too -- the receipt correctly proves what ggen wrote, but 'Pass' here should not be read as 'the generated code is valid Rust/TOML/etc.'; ggen has no such check in its declarative-rules write path.
